entry.title: Disable Double-Shift keymap for everything search in Jetbrains' IDE
entry.content: Search everywhere is not a useless function, however, it use double shift as the short-cut.I always mistakenly triggering it when I toggle letter case, which is annoying. Finally I found its solution on StackOverflow. take IDEA as an example open the IDE, press Ctrl+Shift+A and find ide.suppress.double.click.handlercheck the box, then the double-shift short-cut will be disabled

entry.title: Aditional notes about operator overloading
entry.content: It is the additional notes about operator overloading in C++, I will skip the basic operate, just list some tips that should draw attention. Overloading I/O operatorsThe purpose is to make it easier like basic data types when we input/output the objects from our own classes. Noticed that definition is declaration minus ‘friend’, don’t use <className>:: Overloading >>12345678//e.g. we define a class named complexfriend istream &operator>>(istream &in, complex &N); //declarationistream &operator>>(istream &in, complex &N){ //definition in>>N.first>>N.second; return in;} Overloading <<1234istream &operator<<(ostream &out, complex &N){ out<<N.first<<N.second; return out;} Overloading subscript operatorTo overloading [], it’s better to define two formats:<type> &operator[](...); and const <type> &operator[](...) const; the second one can help us to suit the const objects. e.g. 123const int & Array::operator[](int i) const{ return elem[i];} Overloading auto-increasing and auto-decreasingOverloading ++ and -- is not complex, but you should pay attention to this tip: It has two positions: pre and post, the specific method is diffrent, as well as the definition. 12345678910111213141516171819// e.g. we define a class named WatchWatch operator++(); //pre formWatch operator++(int); //post formWatch Watch::self_add(){ ++second; return \*this;}Watch & Watch::operator++(){ return self_add();}Watch & Watch::operator++(int){ Watch pre = \*this; self_add(); return pre;}

entry.title: Explain Input and Output Streams in C++
entry.content: After learning the basic C++ grammar, I still confuse on some input operations, so I tried to write this note, talking about some specific things about istream and ostream in C++. These pictures shows the relationship between some Stream Classes. Input Streamuse array form to asist inputInput one item1234567891011#include "iostream"using namespace std;int main(){ int i{}; char c{}; cin >> i; cin >> c;}-----------//only read first two items Input specific number of items12345678910111213141516#include "iostream"using namespace std;int main(){ cin << n; int ints[n]{}; char chars[n]{};}for(int i = 0; i < 5; i++){ cin >> ints;}for(int i = 0; i < 5; i++){ cin >> chars;} get() and getline() methodNone parameter123int c;while((c = cin.get())!=EOF) cout.put(c); One parametercin.get(c) read a character from istream and assign it to c; 1234char c;while(cin.get(c)){ cout.put(c);} Three parameterscin.get(array_name, length, 'end_char') (nums means will read n-1 chars)123char ch[20];cin.get(ch, 10, '\n'); \\the third param default is '\n' if omittedcout<<ch; just remember: it will end if(reach the length||find the endchar) getline() methodgetline() can read a line of input chars, similar to get(ch, num, 'end_char') diffrence between getline() and cin getline() can read blank characters, but cin can’t, it will end reading when meeting blanks getlin() can only read chars, while cin can read every types. Other methods:eof()eof means end of file, we usually use it to judge the end.123456789// remove blanks from a stringint main(){ char c; while (!cin.eof()) { if((c=cin.get())!=' '){ cout.put(c); } }} peek() and putback()peek() will return the next char(only observe it, pointer will not move) putback() will insert specific char to the pointer before the current char1234//Read a char from the stream then put it backchar c;c = cin.get();cin.putback(c); ★ a comprehensive example:1234567891011121314151617181920212223#include "iostream"using namespace std;int main(){char c[20];int ch;cin.getline(c, 15, '/');cout<<"first part is: "<<c<<endl;ch = cin.peek(); //ch is 'c'cout<<ch<<endl;cin.putback(c[3]);cin.getline(c, 15, '/');cout<<"second part is: "<<c<<endl;}----------------------------------//Input: I like/coding and playing badminton//Output: first part is: I like// 99// second part is: icoding and pl----------------------------------//Analyse: firstly, it read '/' and stop, c is 'I like', pointer is at '/', the we use// peek to observe the next char:c, and its ASCII code is 99, the c[3]('i') is// put after the pointer, so the second part is 'icoding and pl' ignore()the function of ignore() is to skip some charscin.ignore(length, 'end_char')1234// RULEif (reach length||see end_char){ end skipping} e.g.if we add cin.ignore(4, 'd'); before the second getline() in the previous example, the second part will be "ding and playi" Output StreamOstream define three objects for outputting: cout cerr clog standard output stream standard error stream standard error stream cerrthe information in cerr object can only display on the screen, it won’t affact the memory file.cerr is always used to output error information quickly. cloginformation in cerr won’t go through buffer, but clog information will be put in, when the buffer is full or meet endl, clog information will be displayed. coutcout has some useful function to control the output formats: functions description setprecision(n) set precision of nums setw(n) set width setfill(c) set the filling char setioflags() set format resetioflags() reset format other functions will be talked about later.

entry.title: Xpath and CSS selector
entry.content: Xpath Selector Expression Description nodename select nodes with the name . select the current node .. select the parant of current node / select from root node // select from this node no matter where they are @ select attributes * match any elem node @* match any attribute node Node predicate Description [num] - [last()] - [position()] - [@id=””] - [@class=””] - CSS Selector Selector Example Description .class .nav select elems whose class is “nav” #id #fruit select elems whose id is “fruit” * * select all elems elem p select <p> elems elem, elem div, p select <div> elems and <p> elems elem elem div p select <p> elems inside <div> elems elem>elem div > p select <p> elems whose parent is <div> [attribute] [target] select elems with a target attrtbute [attr~=v] [title~=flower] whose title contains flower [attr^=v] a[href^=”https”] whose href begins with “https” [attr$=v] a[href$=”com”] whose href ends with “com” [attr*=v] a[href*=”engine”] whose href contains “engine”

entry.title: How to build a simple django project
entry.content: 0. Prefaceinstall python 3.6install pycharm professional 2018.2install django 2.1 1. Create the projectcmd input django-admin startproject <project_name> to start a project if your project name is my_blog, just input django-admin startproject my_blog 2. Run the project to testcmd input python manage.py runserver to run it 123456<project_name> folder:- `wsgi.py`:Python webserver gateway interface- `urls.py`:configurate the urls- `settings.py`:some settings- `__init__.py`:define as a package,you can just import `my_blog``manage.py`: 3. Create the first app:cmd inputdjango-admin startapp <app_name> to start an applications if your app name is blog, just input django-admin startproject blog don not forget to add it to settings.py!!!!!!!!!(this simple mistake cost me 3 hours to find a css mistake solution) 123456789blog folder:- migration folder -> `init.py`:something about migration- `init.py`- `admin.py`:back stage management module for this app- `apps.py`:settings about this app- `models.py`:database module *- `tests.py`:automated testing *- `views.py`:responsive logic 4. Create the first page:blog->views.py:(to write the page) 1234567#a very simple return method:from django.shortcuts import renderfrom django.http import HttpResponse#function name is not require, ordinary using request as the parameterdef index(request): return HttpResponse("Hello world!") my_blog->urls.py:(this method just a simple test, which is deprecated) 12345#add this to the headfrom <app_name> import views#add this to the urlpatterns[]path('\index', views.index) (the url pattern include three parameters:URL(RegEx),method and name) execute the second step to runserver, don’t forget to add /index to the end of the url to see your app page:Hello world 5. Enhance the url configurationthe method in step 4 is clumsy when facing a crowd of urls, this method will handle this problem: my_blog->urls.py:12345#add the include methodfrom django.urls import path, include#add a url in urlpatterns:path('blog/', include('blog.urls')), new a urls.py python file in blog folder, and write these(like the step 4): 1234567#this time we use re_path to use Regexfrom django.urls import re_pathfrom . import viewsurlpatterns = [ re_path(r'^$',views.index),] then open http://127.0.0.1:8000/index/ you will see the page. (if you want to make another page from the app, such as http://127.0.0.1:8000/index/article_1, remember the Regex should be re_path(r'article_1/$'), don’t forget the last /) then the url-logic will be more explicit 6.Write a templateDjango has its own template language: Django-Template-Language(DTL) we can also choose other language by changing the settings.py new a folder named ‘template’ in blog folder new a HTML file in the template folder, for example: index.html(write something) then change the settings.py file to fill the path of templates: 12345#e.g.TEMPLATES = [ { 'DIRS': ['C:/Users/Kevin/my_blog/blog/templates'], } you should change to the render method to get the page:blog->views.py 12345#django has imported render automatically at firstfrom django.shortcuts import renderdef index(request): return render(request, 'index.html') render has three parameters, the third is a dict parameter,which is for transmission(from backend to the template) you can use double braces in template to use this parameter e.g.blog->views.py1return render(request, 'index.html', {'sayHello':'Welcome to my website!'}) index.html123<body> {{sayhello}}</body> if you want to link the html file with css, just refer to the external css file, or use statics like this: new a folder named statics in blog(just the same level with templates folder), new css and pictures folder in it. add these in html file to link to the specific css:12345<!--add this at the most top-->{% static %}<!--use static to link css--><link rel="stylesheet" href="{% static 'css/main.css' %}"> another question is coming, when we have a plenty of apps, the templates html will be confused by django. so in each app_templates folder we can new folder named that app name, and place html in it. [exercise] try to create another app article 7.Models and DatabaseORM(Object relation mapping) Django has encapsulate the database operation definite a class in the models of an app: blog->models.py 123456from django.db import models# Create your models here.class Article(models.Model): title = models.CharField(max_length=30) content = models.TextField(null=True) generate a data sheet: cmd input:(preparation) $python manage.py makemigrations (app_name)(without app_name, it means each apps will make a migration) (migrate) $python manage.py migrate now a database whose suffix is .sqlite3 will be create in the project root directory, you can manage the database. 8.Data transmissionback end blog->views.py1234567# add modelsfrom . import modelsdef index(request): articles = models.Article.objects.all() #the last one is a method, all() is one of them return render(request, 'blog/index.html', {'articles': articles}) (if use all(), articles will be a list) front end blog->templates->index.html e.g.12345678{% for article in articles %}<div class="title"> {{ article.title }}</div><div class="content"> {{ article.content }}</div>{% endfor %} 9.Admincmd input : $python manage.py createsuperuser(create a superadmin) if you want a Chinese page, revamp the setting.py : LANGUAGE_CODE = 'zh_Hans' let the admin page receive the database we created before: article->admin123from . import modelsadmin.site.register(models.Article) in the admin page, each article use Artical object as their name, it’s quite inconvenient: article->models.py123#these should be added in the classdef __str__(self): return self.title 10.New page developmentFirstly create a new html file in templates folder, just abreast of index.html When we want to click a hyperlink to go to the article_detail page, it’s essential to add a method in blog->views.py: 123def article_page(request, article_id): article = models.Article.objects.get(pk=article_id) return render(request, 'blog/article_page.html', {'article': article}) configure the url blog->urls.py1234#add new urlsurlpatterns=[ re_path(r'^article/(?P<article_id>[0-9]+)$', views.article_page)] e.g. we can visit the first article just through localhost:8000/blog/article/1 11.Configurate the hyperlinkwe want to realize a function that when we click the title of a specific article in the index page, the url will skip to the detial page of this article: my_blog->urls.py1234#add another param in include to identify the nameurlpatterns = [ re_path(r'^blog$',include(('blog.views','blog'),namespace='blog')),] blog->urls.py1234#add a nameurlpatterns=[ re_path(r'^article/(?P<article_id>[0-9]+)$', views.article_page, name='article_page')] modify the index html page:blog->templates->index.html123<!--...--><a href="{% url 'blog:artical_page' article_id%}">{{ article.title }}</a><!--...--> 12.create the edit page create a new html file: blog->templates->blog->edit_page.html123456789101112131415161718...<form action="{% url 'blog:edit_action' %}" method="post"> {% csrf_token %} <label>标题 <span class="ui fluid input"> <input type="text" name="title"> </span> </label> <br> <div class="field"> <label>内容 <span class="ui fluid input"> <textarea name="" id="" col rows="20"></textarea> </span> </label> <a type="submit" value="提交"></a></form>... comfigure a url for edit_page:addre_path(r'^edit/$', views.edit_page, name='edit_page') to urlpatterns comfigure a url for edit_action:addre_path(r'^edit/$', views.edit_action, name='edit_action') to urlpatterns add an action method in blog->views.py123456def edit_action(request): title = request.POST.get('title','TITLE') content = request.POST.get('content','CONTENT') models.Article.objects.create(title=title, content=content) articles = models.Article.objects.all() return render(request, 'blog/index.html', {'articles': articles}) add a hyperlink in ‘index.html’ to create a new article:1<a href="{% url 'blog:edit_page' %}">新建文章</a> The above is how to create a new article, now we need to realize how to modify an existed article: change the urlpatterns:1re_path(r'^edit/(?P<article_id>[0-9]+)$', views.edit_page, name='edit_page'), add a parameter (article_id) to the edit_page method:123456def edit_page(request, article_id): if str(article_id)=='0': return render(request, 'blog/edit_page.html') else: article = models.Article.objects.get(pk=article_id) return render(request, 'blog/edit_page.html', {{'article':article}}) then add an if judgement to the edit_page.html, by the way don’t forget to write another action page(when create a new article, we make article_id 0) 12345678{% if article %}<input type="hidden" name="article_id" value="{{ article.id }}"><!--chaneg the blank value to be article.title/article.content-->...{% else %}<input type="hidden" name="article_id" value=0>...{% endif %} improve edit_action method1234567891011121314def edit_action(request): title = request.POST.get('title', 'TITLE') content = request.POST.get('content', 'CONTENT') article_id = request.POST.get('article_id', 0) if article_id == '0': models.Article.objects.create(title=title, content=content) articles = models.Article.objects.all() return render(request, 'blog/index.html', {'articles': articles}) else: article = models.Article.objects.get(pk=article_id) article.title = title article.content = content article.save() return render(request, 'blog/article_page.html', {'article': article}) All the steps above is a method to set up a primary django project. Thanks for reading.

entry.title: The basic operation of numpy
entry.content: What is numpy?  Here is the definition of numpy library in wikipedia: NumPy/ˈnʌmpaɪ/ is a library for Python, adding support for large, multi-dimensional arrays and matrices, along with a large collection of high-level mathematical functions to operate on these arrays. In a word, numpy can make it more convenient for us to operate array and matrix in Python, with high efficiency. Begin to useFirstly, if you haven’t install this library please use pip install numpy to get it. Typing numpy for many times is a waste, so we usually import numpy as np Numpy Array & Matrix1234567891011# create arraysarr1 = np.array([i for i in range(n)])arr2 = np.zeros(shape=(row_size, col_size), dtype=int)arr3 = np.ones(shape=(row, col))arr4 = np.full(shape=(row, col), fill_value=v)arr5 = np.arange(head, end, step_length)arr6 = np.linspace(head, end, divide_numbers)arr7 = np.random.randint(head, end) # Right open intervalnp.random.seed() # set the seed, if seed is equal then random nums are the samenp.random.random(size=(3, 5))np.random.normal(mean_value, variance, size) # random distribution propeties ndim shape size you can use slide operation to visit elements in an array, such as arr[:2,:4,2] functions1arr.reshape(row, col) [annotation]: if one of the params is determined, another can be set as -1 (means auto distribute) combination123np.concatenate([arr1, arr2], axis=0/1)np.vstack() pile verticallynp.hstack() pile horizontally devide1234arr1, arr2 = np.split(arr, [n], <axis=1>)split arr (position is n)np.vsplit()np.hsplit() Built-in mathematical operations12345np.sin(arr)np.exp(arr)np.abs(arr)np.log<num>(arr) #log(arr) means napierian logarithmnp.exp(3, arr) #means e^x Arithmatics are aimming at each elements in the array/matrix 1234A*B: means every elements in A multiply corresponding elements in BA.dot(B)：means the normal multiplication for matrics, A*BA.T： the transpositionnp.linalg.inv(arr)：inverse matrix of arr Aggregation operation12345678910np.sum(arr)np.max(arr)np.min(arr)# also support for axisnp.prod(arr): multiplication of all the elementsnp.mean(arr): mean valuenp.median(arr): median valuenp.precentile(arr, q=n): the value which position is at n%np.var(): variancenp.std(): standard diviation Index:12np.argmin(arr): return the index of the mininumnp.argmax(arr): sort123np.random.shuffle(arr)：make arr disorderednp.sort(arr)：sortnp.partion(arr, n): quicksort of arr you can add arg tag before the method’s name (e.g. argpartition, argsort), in this way it will return the array/matrix consist of indexs nums It alse support axis param fancy indexing:using slice to select regular eles is enough, using fancy123456ind = np.array([0, 2] [1, 3])arr[ind] # return corresponding indexrow = np.array([0, 1, 2])col = np.array([3, 4, 5])arr[row, col] #return values of (0, 3),(1, 4),(2, 5) logic operations between arrays or matrices will return its bool type 123456np.sum(x < 3) # x<3 will return a bool matrix# this states means returning the nums of elems who < 3np.count_nonzero# literal meaningnp.any(logical operation) # return true if any one is trueny.all(logical operation) # retunr true only when all is true matplotlib (viusualization tool)import matplotlib as mplalways use its pyplot：import matplotlib.pyplot as plt 1234567plt.plot(x, y, color="", linestyle="--", label="")plt.xlim(min, max)plt.axis([xmin,xmax,ymin,ymax])plt.xlabel("xxx")plt.legend() # add represantationplt.title("")plt.show # call at alst to show the pic scatter diagram:1plt.scatter(x, y, alpha=0.5) #alpha is the opaque degree

entry.title: Some issues and their solutions about coursera
entry.content: Coursera is one of the most famous online learning platforms. However, some frustrating things could occur either, here are some issues I met and the solutions of them. Problem1: the video is loading all the time:method:open your hosts file, for most windows users, it’s in C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc add these lines to the bottom of your hosts file and save it 12345678952.84.246.90 d3c33hcgiwev3.cloudfront.net52.84.246.252 d3c33hcgiwev3.cloudfront.net52.84.246.144 d3c33hcgiwev3.cloudfront.net52.84.246.72 d3c33hcgiwev3.cloudfront.net52.84.246.106 d3c33hcgiwev3.cloudfront.net52.84.246.135 d3c33hcgiwev3.cloudfront.net52.84.246.114 d3c33hcgiwev3.cloudfront.net52.84.246.90 d3c33hcgiwev3.cloudfront.net52.84.246.227 d3c33hcgiwev3.cloudfront.net run cmd.exe, input ipconfig/flushdns then press Enter refresh your coursera page, the video will play out successfully. Problem2: the subtitles are too big/smallmethod:inspect the page(by right click to choose or press F12), add video::-webkit-media-text-track-display {font-size: xx%;} to head-><style class="vjs-styles-defaults>" just like this: (you can change the percentage value to find your perfect subtitle size) to be continued…

entry.title: Use Atom as an elegant Markdown editor
entry.content: Atom, I believe most of you may know about this editor. It is a free and open-source text editor developed by GitHub. Atom is not only a source code editor, but an elegant Markdown editor. Here is the interface of my Atom when I use it as a Markdown editor. In this passage, I will show you how I change it to an excellent Markdown editor by using some useful plug-ins in Windows 10. Markdown Preview Enhancedmarkdown-preview-enhanced is an extension that provide some useful functions, including

entry.title: Welcome to my Github Page
entry.content: This personal website was built in Sep 19th, 2018, aiming to store and share my notes. At first, I planed to set up a personal website by using Django frame + jQuery + MySQL + Apache, however, I find it so tough for me to design style and database. I can’t spend so much time to learn these knowledge of front end, and the cost for a damain name and web server is too high for a student. That’s the reason why I give up this idea.(But in the future I will build a dynamic page) Then I thought of building my blog on Github Page, it’s free and easy to set up. Hexo is chosed to be the static website’s generator rather than Jekell or Hugo. After reading some relative documents, this website was set up as my personal blog page. Whoever you are, thanks for visiting my blog. I hope some of my articles can be useful to you.
